title: 3 Settlement, Reports, and Reconciliation of International Transfers
body_text:
3	Settlement, Reports, and Reconciliation of International Transfers
3.1	Settlement of International Transfers
Settlement for International Transfers between Central 1 and your financial institution is the same as electronic bill payments. See the Electronic Bill Payments System Part for more information.
Settlement to the recipientâs account typically takes 1â3 business days, but it may differ by country based on local delivery times.
Note: 	Settlement to the customerâs account is always in Canadian dollars, even if the customer has a US dollar account. The customerâs Canadian dollar account is displayed as the only sending account option when creating an international transfer on the Agility Forex website.
3.2	Foreign Exchange Spread Revenue
Central 1 credits your foreign exchange spread revenue to your Canadian dollar Central 1 account on the 15th of the following month, using transaction code âSDâ (Treasury Note). See Transactions and Charge Codes, Form 3667. If the 15th falls on a weekend or statutory holiday, Central 1 credits your account the next business day.
If you donât have a Central 1 account, settlement is processed via AFT. A statement providing the number of transactions, the amount (in Canadian dollars), and the revenue generated is uploaded to Central 1âs Treasury Reports when the credit is processed. See the Treasury Reports User Guide for information on accessing Treasury Reports.
Related Information
Treasury Reports User Guide,
Form 6158
3.2	Reports for International Transfers
Since international transfers are processed via the Electronic Bill Payment System, they are included on Central 1âs Electronic Bill Payment System reports. See the Electronic Bill Payments System Part for information on bill payment reports.
3.3	Reconciliation of International Transfers
Since international transfers are processed via the Electronic Bill Payment System, they are reconciled like other bill payment transactions. Subscribing financial institutions and provincial centrals can use the HREC file for reconciling international transfers. See the Electronic Bill Payments System Part and HREC File Specifications, Form 6118, for more information.
Related Information
HREC File Specifications,
Form 6118
END
